sentences.english: Hello and welcome to The English We Speak, where we explain phrases used by fluent English speakers so that you can use them too.
sentences.korean: 안녕하세요, 유창한 영어 화자들이 쓰는 표현을 설명하는 The English We Speak에 오신 것을 환영합니다.

sentences.english: I'm Feifei, and I'm here with Phil.
sentences.korean: 저는 페이페이이고, 필과 함께합니다.

sentences.english: Hello. How are you, Feifei?
sentences.korean: 안녕. 잘 지내, 페이페이?

sentences.english: Did you buy that new car that you wanted?
sentences.korean: 원하던 새 차 샀어?

sentences.english: No, actually, I didn't.
sentences.korean: 아니, 사실 안 샀어.

quiz.question: What does '나타나다, 찾아오다' mean in English?

sentences.english: I went to look, but there wasn't anything at the right price that I liked.
sentences.korean: 보러 갔는데 마음에 드는 적정 가격대가 없었어.

sentences.english: But that doesn't matter - it's not an emergency, I can bide my time until the right deal comes along.
sentences.korean: 하지만 괜찮아 — 급한 건 아니니까 좋은 거래가 올 때까지 기다릴 수 있어.

sentences.english: That sounds like smart thinking!
sentences.korean: 현명한 생각이네!

sentences.english: And that's the phrase we're looking at in this episode - 'bide someone's time' - and when we're speaking it can be 'bide my time', 'bide your time', 'bide his or her time', 'bide our time' or 'bide their time'.
sentences.korean: 이번 에피소드에서 다룰 표현이야 — 'bide someone's time'. 말할 때는 'bide my time', 'bide your time', 'bide his or her time' 등으로 쓸 수 있어.

sentences.english: It means to wait for an opportunity.
sentences.korean: 기회를 기다린다는 뜻이야.

quiz.question: What does '인내심 있게' mean in English?

sentences.english: What can you say about it?
sentences.korean: 이에 대해 뭐라고 할 수 있어?

sentences.english: 'Bide' is a very old-fashioned word that means wait.
sentences.korean: 'bide'는 '기다리다'라는 뜻의 매우 구식 단어야.

sentences.english: We don't really use it on its own anymore.
sentences.korean: 이제 단독으로는 잘 안 쓰지만, 'to bide your time'이라는 표현에서 살아남았어.

sentences.english: But it survives in this expression 'to bide your time', meaning to wait patiently for the right moment or opportunity.
sentences.korean: 적절한 순간이나 기회를 인내심 있게 기다린다는 뜻이야.

sentences.english: You talked about biding your time for the right deal to come along - you don't need a new car right now, you're happy to wait.
sentences.korean: 좋은 거래가 올 때까지 기다린다고 했지 — 지금 당장 새 차가 필요하진 않으니까 기꺼이 기다리는 거야.

quiz.question: What does '때를 기다리다' mean in English?

sentences.english: And we can use it anywhere where opportunities come up.
sentences.korean: 기회가 생기는 어떤 상황에서든 쓸 수 있어.

sentences.english: Maybe at work, you're not totally happy with your job, but rather than look for a new one, you're happy to bide your time until there's an opportunity for a promotion.
sentences.korean: 직장에서 현재 일에 완전히 만족하지 않더라도, 새 일을 찾기보다 승진 기회가 올 때까지 기꺼이 기다리는 거야.

sentences.english: Yes, so you're waiting patiently for the right job to come up.
sentences.korean: 맞아, 적합한 일이 나올 때까지 인내심 있게 기다리는 거야.

sentences.english: Let's listen to some examples of people using 'bide someone's time'.
sentences.korean: 사람들이 쓰는 예시를 들어보자.

sentences.english: My sister auditions for this dance company every year and she still hasn't got in, but she's biding her time, practising her skills, and hopefully she'll get in soon.
sentences.korean: 언니가 이 댄스 컴퍼니에 매년 오디션을 보는데 아직 들어가지 못했어. 하지만 기다리면서 실력을 연습하고 있고, 곧 들어갈 수 있길 바라고 있어.

quiz.question: What does '나타나다, 찾아오다' mean in English?

sentences.english: I really want to go to the beach with my friend, but it keeps raining every weekend.
sentences.korean: 친구와 해변에 정말 가고 싶은데, 매주 주말마다 비가 와.

sentences.english: We've just got to bide our time and wait for a sunny day.
sentences.korean: 맑은 날이 올 때까지 참고 기다려야 해.

sentences.english: I've just learnt the secret to fishing.
sentences.korean: 낚시의 비결을 방금 알았어.

sentences.english: You've just got to bide your time until a big one comes along.
sentences.korean: 큰 놈이 올 때까지 참고 기다리면 돼.

sentences.english: You're listening to The English We Speak, and we're talking about the phrase 'bide your time'.
sentences.korean: The English We Speak을 듣고 계시고, 'bide your time'에 대해 이야기하고 있어요.

quiz.question: What does '나타나다, 찾아오다' mean in English?

sentences.english: Remember, it means to wait patiently for the right opportunity.
sentences.korean: 적절한 기회를 인내심 있게 기다린다는 뜻이에요.

sentences.english: And Phil, there are no negative associations with this phrase, are there?
sentences.korean: 필, 이 표현에 부정적 의미는 없죠?

sentences.english: No, it's all about patience and waiting for a good opportunity.
sentences.korean: 아니, 인내심과 좋은 기회를 기다리는 것에 관한 거야.

sentences.english: So, Feifei, I hope the right car comes along for you soon!
sentences.korean: 페이페이, 곧 맞는 차가 나오길 바라!

sentences.english: Thank you! See you next time!
sentences.korean: 고마워! 다음에 봐!

sentences.english: Bye!
sentences.korean: 안녕!
